task_completion.description: Configure the rocket with a 24mm motor mount and an Estes E12-6 motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Inner Tube configuration window showing an inner diameter of 24mm (or ~0.945 inches) and the 'Motors and Configuration' tab showing an 'Estes E12-6' motor assigned to the mount.

task_completion.description: Add internal mass components (ballast) to tune the apogee.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Mass component' or 'Ballast' item added to the rocket design tree in the upper-left panel of the OpenRocket main window.

task_completion.description: Adjust the parachute diameter to tune the flight time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Parachute configuration window being open with a specific diameter entered, or the parachute component being actively modified in the design tree.

task_completion.description: Run a simulation achieving the target apogee (244m ± 5m) and flight time (45.0s ± 2.0s).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket showing a completed simulation run with an Apogee between 239m and 249m, and a Flight time between 43.0s and 47.0s.

task_completion.description: Save the calibrated rocket to tarc_rocket.ork.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket window title bar displaying 'tarc_rocket.ork' or a file manager/save dialog showing the file saved in /home/ga/Documents/rockets/.

task_completion.description: Write a calibration report containing the final simulated apogee, flight time, ballast mass, and parachute diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor window showing a file named 'tarc_report.txt' (saved in /home/ga/Documents/exports/) that explicitly lists the final apogee, flight time, ballast mass, and parachute diameter.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to perform the calibration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (design tree, configuration windows, simulation tab) rather than just terminal commands modifying the XML of the .ork file.

integrity.description: The reported values come from actual simulation runs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the apogee and flight time written in the text report match the actual results displayed in the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate or copy the target numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not alter the lengths or diameters of the body tube, nose cone, or fins.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D/3D rocket preview in OpenRocket; the rocket should maintain its original standard proportions without bizarrely stretched or shrunken external components.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Apogee: 244.0m (± 5.0m). Target Flight Time: 45.0s (± 2.0s). The calibrated rocket must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/tarc_rocket.ork. The calibration report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/tarc_report.txt and must contain the simulated apogee, flight time, ballast mass, and parachute diameter.
